typescript.reason: devDependencies are dropped by default — npm/yarn/pnpm parity.

react.reason: peerDependencies are dropped by default — npm/yarn/pnpm parity. Its transitives (loose-envify, js-tokens) therefore do not appear either.

fsevents.reason: optionalDependencies are dropped by default — npm/yarn/pnpm parity.

mend_expected_behavior.trusted_dependencies_note: trustedDependencies is an install-script allowlist only. It has no effect on dep-tree shape. 'esbuild' is listed but is NOT a direct dep — Mend must not invent a dep entry for it.
known_failure_modes: Mend keeps peerDependencies (react) and/or optionalDependencies (fsevents) instead of dropping them, inflating the direct set beyond hono — tracked as SCA-5896. Bun's resolver defaults diverge from npm/yarn/pnpm here.
Mend keeps devDependencies (typescript) instead of dropping it.
Mend misreads trustedDependencies as a dependency list and emits a ghost 'esbuild' dep.
Mend crashes on JSONC trailing commas in bun.lock before reaching any package entry.

warnings: EXPECTED BEHAVIOR (npm/yarn/pnpm parity): Mend keeps ONLY regular `dependencies` (hono) and drops devDependencies (typescript), peerDependencies (react) and optionalDependencies (fsevents). hono has no dependencies, so the correct tree is 1 direct / 0 transitive / 1 total.
Until the Bun dep-group parity fix ships, Mend over-reports by keeping peer+optional (and possibly dev) deps. This divergence is tracked as SCA-5896; the autotest is marked xfail against it.
Bun is NOT a UA-supported resolver. Mend attempts npm-resolver fallback against bun.lock. JSONC comments and trailing commas may cause parse failures depending on Mend UA version.

trustedDependencies lists 'esbuild' which has no corresponding package in this manifest or lockfile. Mend must ignore unknown trust entries — if a ghost 'esbuild' dep appears in the output tree, that is a false positive.
